boundary: P6 is a growth-risk and completion-requirement ledger.  It does not prove full high-energy softness, and it does not by itself force a string/tower UV completion.

high_energy_targets.finite_vector_target: no uncancelled longitudinal growth such as A ~ s/M_X^2 or A ~ s^2/M_X^4
high_energy_targets.contact_boundary: four-fermion contact terms are EFT data, not UV softness proofs
high_energy_targets.tower_condition: tower-like UV completion becomes motivated only if finite Goldstone/Higgs/source completions fail or if stronger Regge-like softness is demanded

summary.tower_like_uv_status: not forced at P6; retained as a comparison class if finite-pole completion fails

transition_growth_summary.interpretation: Every broken/off-face current needs a generalized Ward completion before longitudinal high-energy behavior can be judged.

vector_branch_audits.naive_high_energy_growth_risk: uncancelled longitudinal piece can scale as s/M_X^2 or worse
required_finite_completion_data: explicit broken generator and current normalization
broken-vector mass relation
Goldstone amplitude phi_X
Higgs/source sector enforcing p_mu M^mu(V_X)=M_X M(phi_X)
spin numerator and contact terms fixed by the same symmetry
vector_branch_audits.tower_like_uv_hint: not forced by P6 alone; becomes relevant if no finite Goldstone/Higgs/source completion cancels the growth

remaining_checks: perturbative unitarity of any induced higher-dimension contact operator
scalar_auxiliary_branch_audits.boundary: P6 does not declare scalar exchange safe phenomenologically; it only says the broken-vector longitudinal Ward problem is absent.

contact_growth_summary.dimension_six_contact_scaling: A_contact ~ s/Lambda^2 in a four-fermion EFT normalization
contact_growth_summary.required_resolution: A contact term may parameterize low-energy matching, but high-energy behavior requires explicit pole, symmetry cancellation, or tower-like UV data.

candidate_implications.p5_status: anomaly-consistent but fails the single irreducible family object criterion
candidate_implications.p6_implication: Finite field-theory completion is possible in principle, but X/Y broken-vector exchange must come with an SU(5)-breaking Higgs/Goldstone sector and proton-channel matching.
candidate_implications.tower_like_uv_role: not indicated before finite SU(5) completion is tested

candidate_implications.p5_status: anomaly-consistent and physically natural, but fails the simple-group and single-object filters
candidate_implications.p6_implication: Leptoquark and SU(2)_R broken currents require product-group breaking data; high-energy closure cannot be judged from isolated poles.
candidate_implications.tower_like_uv_role: not indicated before finite product-group completion is tested

candidate_implications.p5_status: standard minimal anomaly-safe single-object survivor under the stated Route-C pre-filter
candidate_implications.p6_implication: The standard Route-C minimal single-object survivor still needs the full broken D5 generator, mass, Goldstone, and Higgs/source sector for longitudinal-vector cancellations.
candidate_implications.tower_like_uv_role: not indicated if the finite Spin(10) action-level completion closes

candidate_implications.p5_status: anomaly-consistent, but not minimal and requires an extra-state lifting audit
candidate_implications.p6_implication: Extra 10+1 states may participate in high-energy cancellations, but then exotics lifting and threshold audits become part of the same branch.
candidate_implications.tower_like_uv_role: possible comparison only after finite E6 plus lifting audit

candidate_implications.p5_status: UV-completion class with stronger high-energy softness/tower structure, but requires compactification-specific spectrum and exotics audits
candidate_implications.p6_implication: A string-derived branch can supply an infinite tower and stronger Regge-like softness, but this requires compactification-specific spectrum and consistency data.
candidate_implications.tower_like_uv_role: serious comparison point if finite-pole GUT branches cannot satisfy controlled high-energy behavior
